title: Why Cracker Barrel Rolled Higher Today
author: Rich Smith
description: Cracker Barrel stock surged 27.6% after reporting better-than-expected Q3 2026 earnings, posting a $0.29 per share profit instead of the predicted $0.42 loss. While same-store sales declined 2.6% year-over-year, GAAP earnings jumped 239% to $1.90 per share, boosted by a $47.4 million settlement from credit card interchange fee litigation. Management also provided optimistic guidance with lower expected inflation in commodities and wages.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rallied 27.6% on earnings beat with unexpected profitability, strong GAAP earnings growth of 239%, and improved full-year guidance. Despite same-store sales declines, lower expected wage and commodity inflation provides positive outlook for future profitability.

title: Cracker Barrel Stock Climbs On Strong Q3 Earnings Result
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL) reported better-than-expected Q3 earnings with revenue of $797.37 million beating estimates and adjusted EPS of 29 cents versus expected loss of 45 cents. The company raised FY26 revenue guidance to $3.27-$3.30 billion and adjusted EBITDA outlook to $120-$125 million. Wells Fargo upgraded the stock from Equal-Weight to Overweight with a price target of $50. Shares surged 29.74% following the announcement.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat revenue and EPS expectations, raised full-year guidance above analyst estimates, demonstrated effective cost management and operational improvements, and received an analyst upgrade from Equal-Weight to Overweight with a significant price target increase from $35 to $50. Stock price surged 29.74% on the results.

title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow Futures Fall As May Inflation Rises The Most In 37 Months— Autozi Internet, Cracker Barrel In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday following geopolitical tensions as Trump ordered military strikes against Iran. The S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Nasdaq 100 all fell in premarket trading. Investors await the May CPI release expected to show a 4.2% year-over-year increase. The Fed is projected to hold interest rates steady in June. Several stocks moved notably: Autozi Internet surged 54% on funding news, while Super Micro Computer dropped 8.74% after announcing a $7 billion offering. Casey's and Cracker Barrel beat earnings expectations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Index declined 0.54% in premarket trading due to geopolitical tensions from Trump's Iran strikes and mixed economic outlook
Index declined 0.88% in premarket; tech stocks underperformed despite AI theme dominance

Stock dropped 8.74% after announcing a $7 billion offering to fund AI server component purchases, diluting existing shareholders

title: Cracker Barrel, Grandpa Joe’s Add Gardners Candies Amid New Product Rollouts
author: Brad Ritter
description: Gardners Candies is expanding its national distribution, adding new products and packaging, with plans to sell in Cracker Barrel stores and multiple retail chains across the United States.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a new retail partner for Gardners Candies' product distribution

title: Next Week’s Earnings to Test Impact of Tariffs on Consumer Spending
author: Bruce Smith
description: Upcoming earnings reports from FedEx, General Mills, Lennar, and restaurant companies will provide insights into consumer sentiment, economic growth, and the potential impact of tariffs on consumer spending.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Year-to-date stock performance is down 18.3%, analysts have lowered sales estimates, and consensus suggests flat revenue growth
Shares down over 20% this year, company forecasts 10-15% earnings per share decline, and facing potential margin pressure from tariffs
